economic_notes: Band operates multiple government business enterprises including Smelqmix Investments LP, Lower Similkameen Community Forests Ltd, Skulqalt Forestry Ltd, and several holding companies. Forestry and property tax revenues noted. Copper Mountain Mine accounts receivable of $1,035,550 suggests resource industry relationship.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 — not 2024. Prior year (2021) figures were restated due to transfer of ownership in Smelqmix Investment LP and infrastructure amortization corrections; accumulated surplus restated from $21,133,177 to $21,609,389. Seven entities accounted for on modified equity basis (GBEs). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $5,270,995 + CMHC $128,392 + Environment Canada $437,000 + FNESC $961,828 + FNHA $1,405,033 + Province of BC $4,295,895 + ONA $156,487 = $12,655,630; OSR includes forestry $555,540 + interest $65,194 + property tax $280,037 + referrals $160,877 + rental $315,425 + insurance proceeds $103,000 + other $461,341 = $1,941,414. No schedule of remuneration included. Chief and Council expenses totalled $376,080. Annual debt service reflects principal repayments only per cash flow statement.
